questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:The process of eudiometry is used to calculate the volume of the various reacting gases present together in a closed vessel, it is done by sparking the content and noting the volume change or pressure change depending upon the conditions mentioned. Various reagents like alkaline pyrogallol, turpentine oil, caustic potash, conc. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ are used to absorb the selective gases like $\mathrm{O}_2, \mathrm{O}_3, \mathrm{CO}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ (v) respectively. A 2.0 mole mixture of $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{He}(\mathrm{g})$ are placed together in a closed container at pressure equal to 50 atm . An electric spark is passed and pressure is noted as 12.5 atm after cooling the content to original temp. (room temp). Oxygen gas is introduced for pressure to change to 25 atm , keeping volume and temp constant. Again electrical spark is passed and pressure drops to 10 atm under original temp. conditions.Question: The moles of $\mathrm{He}(\mathrm{g})$ present in the mixture sample is
questions.solution:
Let the no. of moles of $\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{O}_2$ and He are $\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}$ and z respectively
$\therefore x+y+z=2.0$
Pressure exerted by 2 moles of gaseous mixture is 50 atm .
After the first electric spark decrease in pressure $=50-12.5=37.5 \mathrm{~atm}$ $\therefore$ decrease in no. of moles of gaseous mixture is 1.5
But from the given information limiting reagent in first step is $\mathrm{O}_2$
$\therefore$ from $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\ell)$
$\therefore \mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_2$ reacted are in the ratio of $2: 1$
$\therefore 0.5$ mole of $\mathrm{O}_2$ should be reacted with 1.0 mole of $\mathrm{H}_2$
$\therefore$ No. of mole of $\mathrm{O}_2$ in the mixture $(\mathrm{y})=0.5$
When again $\mathrm{O}_2$ is passed, pressure increased from 12.5 to 25.0 atm
$\therefore$ Change in pressure $=12.5 \mathrm{~atm}$
$\therefore$ No. of moles of $\mathrm{O}_2$ added $=\frac{12.5 \times 2}{50}=0.5 \mathrm{~mole}$
Now $\mathrm{H}_2$ will be completely reacted No. of moles $\mathrm{H}_2$ actually left after first electric spark $=x-1$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{H}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ & (x-1)\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right)\end{aligned}$
Now change in pressure in $25-10=15 \mathrm{~atm}$ $\therefore$ change in no. of moles is $\frac{15 \times 2}{50}=0.6$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore(\mathrm{x}-1)+\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}-1}{2}\right)=0.6 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=1.4\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ no. of moles of $\mathrm{H}_2=1.4$
$\therefore$ no. of moles of $\mathrm{He}=2.0-(1.4+0.5)=0.1$
$\therefore$ mole fraction of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{1.4}{2.0}=0.7$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Paragraph According to Maxwell distribution law, the probability distribution function is given as below: $\mathbf{F}(\mathrm{v})=4 \pi\left(\frac{\mathrm{M}}{2 \pi \mathbf{R T}}\right)^{3 / 2} \mathrm{v}^2 \mathrm{e}^{-M \mathrm{v}^2 / 2 R \mathrm{~T}}$ M-molar mass of gas, v-speed, R-gas constant, T-Absolute temperature Fraction of molecule in the range of $v_1$ to $v_2=\int_{v_1}^{v_2} f(v) d v$Question Average speed ( $\mathrm{V}_{\text {avg }}$ ) most probable speed ( $\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{mp}}$ ) and root mean square ( $\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{ms}}$ ) speeds can be derived from Maxwell distribution. Choose the incorrect option regarding molecular speeds
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph According to Maxwell distribution law, the probability distribution function is given as below: $\mathbf{F}(\mathrm{v})=4 \pi\left(\frac{\mathrm{M}}{2 \pi \mathbf{R T}}\right)^{3 / 2} \mathrm{v}^2 \mathrm{e}^{-M \mathrm{v}^2 / 2 R \mathrm{~T}}$ M-molar mass of gas, v-speed, R-gas constant, T-Absolute temperature Fraction of molecule in the range of $v_1$ to $v_2=\int_{v_1}^{v_2} f(v) d v$Question Select the incorrect statement about a gas following Maxwell distribution law.
options: Exponential term implies that the fraction of molecules with very high speed will be very small
$\frac{\mathrm{M}}{2 R T}$ in exponent implies that heavy molecules are unlikely to be found with very high speed
$\mathbf{v}^2$ in pre-exponential term implies very slow molecules will be very small
$\frac{\mathrm{M}}{2 \mathrm{RT}}$ in pre-exponential term implies that heavy molecules are unlikely to be found with very high speeds
questions.solution: Remaining part of the function will make the overall fraction $=1$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: 1 mole of a real gas is taken in a container at T kelvin, its density, $d$ varies with pressure as $d=3 P-0.6 P^2$. Where $d$ is expressed in gm/L and $\mathbf{P}$ is expressed in atm. Answer the following questions.Question: When 1 mole of the above gas attains maximum density at the same temperature, then volume of the container (in litre) in terms of molar mass $(\mathrm{M})\left(\mathrm{gm} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right)$ of the given by

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Paragraph:The process of eudiometry is used to calculate the volume of the various reacting gases present together in a closed vessel, it is done by sparking the content and noting the volume change or pressure change depending upon the conditions mentioned. Various reagents like alkaline pyrogallol, turpentine oil, caustic potash, conc. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ are used to absorb the selective gases like $\mathrm{O}_2, \mathrm{O}_3, \mathrm{CO}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ (v) respectively. A 2.0 mole mixture of $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{He}(\mathrm{g})$ are placed together in a closed container at pressure equal to 50 atm . An electric spark is passed and pressure is noted as 12.5 atm after cooling the content to original temp. (room temp). Oxygen gas is introduced for pressure to change to 25 atm , keeping volume and temp constant. Again electrical spark is passed and pressure drops to 10 atm under original temp. conditions.Question: The mole fraction of $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ present in the initial mixture is:
questions.solution:
Let the no. of moles of $\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{O}_2$ and He are $\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y}$ and z respectively
$\therefore x+y+z=2.0$
Pressure exerted by 2 moles of gaseous mixture is 50 atm .
After the first electric spark decrease in pressure $=50-12.5=37.5 \mathrm{~atm}$ $\therefore$ decrease in no. of moles of gaseous mixture is 1.5
But from the given information limiting reagent in first step is $\mathrm{O}_2$
$\therefore$ from $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\ell)$
$\therefore \mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_2$ reacted are in the ratio of $2: 1$
$\therefore 0.5$ mole of $\mathrm{O}_2$ should be reacted with 1.0 mole of $\mathrm{H}_2$
$\therefore$ No. of mole of $\mathrm{O}_2$ in the mixture $(\mathrm{y})=0.5$
When again $\mathrm{O}_2$ is passed, pressure increased from 12.5 to 25.0 atm
$\therefore$ Change in pressure $=12.5 \mathrm{~atm}$
$\therefore$ No. of moles of $\mathrm{O}_2$ added $=\frac{12.5 \times 2}{50}=0.5 \mathrm{~mole}$
Now $\mathrm{H}_2$ will be completely reacted No. of moles $\mathrm{H}_2$ actually left after first electric spark $=x-1$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{H}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ & (x-1)\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right)\end{aligned}$
Now change in pressure in $25-10=15 \mathrm{~atm}$ $\therefore$ change in no. of moles is $\frac{15 \times 2}{50}=0.6$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore(\mathrm{x}-1)+\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}-1}{2}\right)=0.6 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=1.4\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ no. of moles of $\mathrm{H}_2=1.4$
$\therefore$ no. of moles of $\mathrm{He}=2.0-(1.4+0.5)=0.1$
$\therefore$ mole fraction of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{1.4}{2.0}=0.7$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: 1 mole of a real gas is taken in a container at T kelvin, its density, $d$ varies with pressure as $d=3 P-0.6 P^2$. Where $d$ is expressed in gm/L and $\mathbf{P}$ is expressed in atm. Answer the following questions.Question: Molecular mass (M) of the gas is given as
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{d}{P}=3-0.6 \mathrm{P} \\ & \text { It }\left(\frac{d}{P}\right)=3=\frac{M}{R T} ; M=3 R T \\ & P \rightarrow 0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
